title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow Futures Inch Up As US-Iran Deal Awaits Trump Approval — Dell, Agilent, Hewlett Packard Enterprise In Focus
author: Benzinga Staff Writer
description: U.S. stock futures edged up on Friday as investors monitored Iran ceasefire developments awaiting Trump approval. Major indexes posted modest gains with healthcare, IT, and consumer discretionary leading. Dell surged nearly 40% on strong earnings, while Agilent and Hewlett Packard Enterprise also gained. Costco reported mixed results with earnings slightly below estimates. The Fed is expected to hold rates steady in June with 98.9% probability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged nearly 40% in pre-market after reporting 88% year-over-year revenue surge to $43.84 billion, the company's fastest revenue growth in eight years. Strong momentum rank across all timeframes.
Shares surged over 18% after first-quarter results beat Wall Street estimates with revenue of $1.84 billion versus estimated $1.80 billion. Strong short and medium-term price trends.

Mixed third-quarter earnings with EPS of $4.93 slightly below consensus of $4.98, though revenue beat estimates at $70.53 billion. Shares fell 0.42% in pre-market. Strong long-term price trend but mixed near-term performance.
Shares surged nearly 20% pre-market as Dell's strong results turned Wall Street bullish on HPE's prospects ahead of its second-quarter earnings next week. Strong price trends across all timeframes.
Shares surged nearly 20% pre-market as Dell's strong results turned Wall Street bullish on HPE's prospects ahead of its second-quarter earnings next week. Strong price trends across all timeframes.

First-quarter revenue grew 33% year-over-year to $1.39 billion with EPS of $0.39. Announced $6 billion multi-year spending commitment with Amazon Web Services. Shares up 1.38% in pre-market with healthy momentum rank.
Up 0.12% in pre-market trading, reflecting modest gains across major indexes. Broad market exposure with slight upward momentum.

title: Why Agilent Technologies Stock Triumphed on Thursday
author: Eric Volkman
description: Agilent Technologies stock surged 17.25% on Thursday following strong Q2 2026 earnings results. The company reported $1.83 billion in revenue (up 10% YoY) and $1.49 per share in adjusted net income (up 14% YoY), beating analyst expectations on both metrics. All three business segments showed growth, with Applied Markets Group leading at 14%. Management raised full-year 2026 guidance for both revenue and adjusted net income.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company beat analyst estimates on both revenue and earnings per share, demonstrated broad-based growth across all three business segments, and raised full-year 2026 guidance for both revenue and adjusted net income, indicating strong operational momentum and management confidence.

title: S&P 500 Hits Record Highs, Snowflake Jumps 37% On AI Boom: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks reached record highs on Thursday following geopolitical news of a ceasefire extension. The S&P 500 advanced 0.5% to 7,557.85, driven by an AI software spending spree. Snowflake surged 37% after beating earnings estimates and announcing a $6 billion AWS partnership expansion. Other notable gainers included Best Buy (+18%), Dollar Tree (+17%), and Agilent Technologies (+17%). However, mixed economic data showed headline PCE inflation at 3.8% while core PCE and consumer spending remained subdued, prompting hawkish Fed commentary.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 37% on blowout earnings and revenue beat, with announcement of $6 billion AWS partnership expansion. Analysts flagged an 'inflection' quarter for AI-cloud orders.
Stock jumped 18% after Q1 sales beat consensus, comparable sales rose 2% YoY, and management reiterated full-year EPS guidance.
Stock climbed 17% after Q1 adjusted EPS beat consensus and company raised FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance.

title: Tissue Diagnostics Market to Reach USD 9.33 Bn by 2031 Driven by Expanding Precision Oncology Applications and Rising Adoption of Digital Pathology, Says Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global tissue diagnostics market is projected to grow from USD 6.45 billion in 2025 to USD 9.33 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 6.35%. Growth is driven by increasing cancer prevalence, rising adoption of precision medicine, advancements in digital pathology and AI-assisted diagnostics, and expanding biomarker-based testing. North America leads the market while Asia-Pacific emerges as a high-growth region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major company in the tissue diagnostics market, which is experiencing strong growth driven by precision oncology and digital pathology adoption. The company benefits from expanding demand for advanced diagnostic solutions.

Identified as a major player in the growing tissue diagnostics market. The company is positioned to benefit from increasing investments in pathology modernization and biomarker-based testing expansion.

Listed among major companies in the tissue diagnostics sector, which is projected to grow at 6.35% CAGR through 2031. The company stands to gain from rising demand for advanced diagnostic reagents and instruments.
Included as a major competitor in the expanding tissue diagnostics market. Benefits from growing adoption of precision medicine and digital pathology technologies.
Listed as a major company in the tissue diagnostics market, which is experiencing growth driven by increasing cancer prevalence and demand for personalized treatment strategies.

title: Global DNA Microarray Market is Predicted to Exhibit Remarkable Growth at a CAGR of ~7% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The global DNA microarray market is expected to grow from USD 2.5 billion in 2025 to USD 4.7 billion by 2034 at a CAGR of ~7%. Growth is driven by rising demand for genomic research, personalized medicine, early disease diagnosis, and increasing prevalence of cancer and genetic disorders. North America leads the market with 41.39% share, while Asia-Pacific emerges as a key growth contributor.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key market player and recently launched the Axiom™ PangenomiX Array in January 2024, demonstrating active product innovation and market expansion in the growing DNA microarray sector.

Identified as a leading company in the market and received European regulatory approval for its SurePrint G3 Human CNV Microarray in May 2025, indicating successful product expansion and market penetration.
Listed among key market players in the growing DNA microarray market, positioned to benefit from the projected 7% CAGR growth through 2034.
Identified as a key player in the expanding DNA microarray market, well-positioned to capture growth from increasing demand for genomic research and personalized medicine applications.
